_meta.note: Master action plan template. Source of truth for new agent onboarding.

steps.taskName: Intro call — understand their goals: cash flow, appreciation, or both? Single family, multi-unit, or condo? Active timeline or just exploring?
Send a quick investment snapshot — avg cap rates in their target area, recent rental demand stats, price-to-rent ratios. Show you speak numbers
"Do you have financing in place already or still working through that? Happy to connect you with a broker who specializes in investment properties"
Send a real example — a property, what it rented for, what it sold for, estimated cash flow. Make the opportunity feel tangible
Follow up — any questions on the numbers? Have they seen anything they liked? Gauge how serious and how fast they move
Follow up — any questions on the numbers? Have they seen anything they liked? Gauge how serious and how fast they move
Market pulse — "Vacancy rates in [neighbourhood] are sitting around X%. Rents up Y% year over year. Strong fundamentals for investors right now"
Are they still actively looking? Any shifts in budget, property type, or target neighbourhood?
Share a recent off-market or quiet listing opportunity — show you bring access they cannot get on their own
"Just helped an investor client pick up a property in [neighbourhood] — tenanted day one. Happy to walk you through how we found it"
6 month check-in — has anything changed? Market timing concern? Financing hurdle? Try to identify the real blocker
"Fall market update — here is what the investment landscape looks like right now vs 6 months ago" with relevant sold data
"Spring market is historically strong for investor purchases before rents reset. Worth a conversation if you are still looking"
1 year follow-up — full reconnect. If still not moving, assess fit and either keep nurturing or move to sphere
Intro — understand their situation (empty nest? retirement? lifestyle change?) and what downsizing means to them
"Downsizing in Toronto — condo vs smaller freehold vs townhouse" breakdown. Show you understand their options
Are you thinking of staying in the same neighbourhood or open to exploring?"
Send 2-3 examples of what their budget could get them in their target area — make it feel real and exciting
Follow up — any questions from the listings? Have they thought about timing tied to their current home sale?
"How to buy and sell at the same time without the stress" — bridge financing, timing strategies
"Just had clients in a similar situation close last month — moved from a 4-bed house to a beautiful condo in [neighbourhood]. Happy to share how we coordinated it"
Any shifts in their thinking? More clarity on timeline or destination neighbourhood?
Neighbourhood spotlight — walkability, amenities, building quality in areas that suit downsizers
"Market update — great inventory right now for [condo/townhouse]. Good time to be a buyer"
6 month check-in — have life circumstances changed? Closer to pulling the trigger?
"What your current home is worth today" — tie their selling opportunity to their buying power
"Spring market is a great time for downsizers — more inventory to choose from. Worth a conversation?"
1 year follow-up — full reconnect
Intro — understand their timeline, reason for selling, and what they need from the process
Send recent comparable sales in their neighbourhood. Show you already know their street
"Quick question — are you thinking of listing this spring/fall or more flexible on timing?"
"What your home could sell for today vs in 60 days with these prep steps" — light staging/prep value-add
Follow up on timeline. Offer to do a walkthrough and give a no-obligation pricing opinion
Share a recent listing you sold (or one nearby) — what it listed for, what it sold for, days on market
Market pulse — "X homes sold on your street/area in the last 30 days. Inventory is [low/moving fast]"
Are they any closer to a decision? Any concerns about the process?
"Spring/fall market is coming — here is why listing now vs waiting could mean $X more"
"Just listed a home nearby — if you want I can show you the marketing package I put together for it"
6 month check-in — re-qualify. Still planning to sell? Timeline shift?
Market update with neighbourhood stats — sold prices, days on market, buyer demand
"Spring market is heating up — great time to talk if you're still considering selling"
1 year follow-up — full reconnect
Intro call — learn their timeline, budget, neighbourhood wishlist. Let them know you specialize in helping FTBs understand the full process
Send FTB process overview — what happens from offer to keys. Position yourself as the guide who makes this less overwhelming
"Have you spoken to a mortgage broker yet? Happy to recommend a couple of great ones if not"
Send neighbourhood breakdown of areas in their budget — show your local knowledge
Check in on mortgage pre-approval status. Share 2-3 active listings that match their criteria
"What to look for at showings" guide — things FTBs miss (HVAC age, electrical panels, etc.)
Quick market update — "X homes sold in [neighbourhood] this month, avg price was $X. Starting to see your budget work well there"
Are they still actively looking? Any shifts in budget or timeline?
Send a recent sale they missed + what it sold for vs asking — shows you're watching the market for them
"Just had a FTB client close last week on a great place in [neighbourhood]. Keeping my eye out for you too"
6 month check-in — has anything changed? Still in the market?
Market update — spring/fall market insight relevant to their search
"Coming up on a year — still thinking about buying? Rates and inventory are looking [X] right now"
1 year follow-up — full reconnect, reassess timeline and motivation
Confirm key exchange time/location with tenant agent
Confirm ALL utility confirmations, key deposit, first and last month received by landlord
Confirm landlord has all signed documents
Arrange pre-closing walkthrough inspection with landlord
Send closing day reminder to landlord
